levels.description: Work may or may not get completed. Processes are either not performed or only partially performed. No goals are satisfied consistently.
characteristics: Ad-hoc and chaotic approach to work
No consistent process followed
Success depends on individual effort
Little or no documentation of processes
Outcomes are unpredictable

levels.description: Work gets completed but is often delayed and over budget. Processes are reactive and unpredictable. Performance depends on the competence and heroics of people rather than proven processes.
characteristics: Processes are ad-hoc and often reactive
Work is completed through individual heroics
Success not consistently repeatable
Schedule and budget overruns are common
Quality is unpredictable

levels.description: Projects are planned, performed, measured, and controlled at the project level. Existing processes are retained during times of stress. Commitments are managed and work products are controlled.
characteristics: Requirements are managed
Projects are planned and executed according to policy
Work products are managed and controlled
Relevant stakeholders are involved
Processes are monitored and corrective actions taken
Status is visible to management at defined points

levels.description: Processes are well characterized, understood, and described in standards, procedures, tools, and methods. The organization's set of standard processes is established and improved over time. Projects establish their defined processes by tailoring the organization's standard processes.
characteristics: Standard processes are documented and maintained
Projects tailor from organizational standards
Processes are proactively managed
Process performance data is collected and analyzed
Training programmes support process deployment
Process assets are maintained and shared

levels.description: The organization and projects establish quantitative objectives for quality and process performance and use them as criteria for managing processes. Quantitative objectives are based on the needs of the customer, end users, organization, and process implementers.
characteristics: Quantitative quality and performance objectives are established
Statistical and quantitative techniques are used
Process performance baselines and models are maintained
Variation in process performance is understood and managed
Special causes of variation are identified and addressed
Outcomes are predictable based on statistical analysis

levels.description: The organization continually improves its processes based on a quantitative understanding of its business objectives and performance needs. The focus is on continually improving process performance through incremental and innovative improvements.
characteristics: Continuous process improvement is embedded in culture
Innovation and technology transfer are pursued
Root causes of defects are systematically identified and resolved
Improvement is driven by quantitative analysis
Lessons learned are deployed across the organization
Process performance is predictable and optimized
